metadata.title:
NEW TERRITORY.


TAKEN IN !

COMMITTEE NAMED TO CUT
1T UP INTO WARDS AND DE-
eide HOW MANY NEW
COUNCILMEN DEMOCRATS
WILL NAME.

pages.raw_text:
The territory which on Nov. 4
voted to be annexed to the city
was formally taken in last night by
the passage of an ordinance to that
effect. Councilmen Brown, Gray,
Funk, Laumer, Pratt and Baldwin
voted in favor of it, and Dalke,
Estep, Lambert and Phillips voted
against it. 1t was noticed that all
the democratic councilmen were
for annexation, and the republicans
against it.


On motion of Councilman Brown
a committee of four, two republi-
cans and two democrats, with Pres.
Pratt, ex-offi clio member, was ap-
pointed for the purpose of ascer-
tanning what would be desirable
boundaries for proposed new wards
and how many new wards were re-
quired. The committee is COM
posed of Councilmen Estep and
Dalke. republicans, and Brown and
Baldwin, democrats.


Although Councilman Funk
when he put his motion seemed Sc





sire that the republicans and
democrats should be given an equal
show on the committee, it was a
foregone conclusion that every
thing is cut and dried for the new
boundaries to be just as the demo
Gratis desire them. Democrats are
in the majority in the council and
this majority will appoint whatso-
ever men it wants as counclmen
of the new wards. Councilman
Estep last night advised that no
new cOuncilmen be appointed, but
they be chosen at the next city
election. Councilman Pratt inform
ed him that under the provisions of
the city charter the present cOun-
Gil has power to appoint new coun-
cilmen who shall hold office until
the next election.
